pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 12 and export_result.sh line 7 both hardcode this path

Task description specifies 'registry' app; export_result.sh line 120 queries 'apps.get_app_config("registry")'; installation scripts reference registry.models (Aircraft, Person, Company); web search confirms Aerobridge uses Django registry app for aircraft and drone management

Aerobridge loads real fixture data (fixtures/initial_data.json with multiple Aircraft, Person, Company, Manufacturer objects with GPS coordinates); Django dumpdata with --indent 2 produces pretty-printed JSON; typical fixture with multiple objects easily exceeds 1024 bytes; verifier.py checks file_size > 100 (line 130), indicating awareness of file size as validity check

privileged_info_summary: Aerobridge is a real, open-source Django-based drone management system developed by OpenSkies (GitHub: openskies-sh/aerobridge). The task exports the 'registry' app, which contains models for Aircraft, Person, Company, Manufacturer, FlightPlan, and related objects. The output is saved to /home/ga/Documents/registry_backup.json in Django JSON fixture format with 2-space indentation. The exported data comes from Aerobridge's real fixture data (fixtures/initial_data.json) which includes persons, aircraft, manufacturers, and operators with GPS coordinates. The minimum file size requirement of 1024 bytes is appropriate for this dataset when pretty-printed.
